context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 20:
﻿Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – Access to project folder denied
Ticket number: TI-1569482
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 03
Owner: Employee 18
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.02.25 09:06
Closure
Closure date: 25.02.25 14:38
Closure text: Root cause identified (group nesting in Entra ID). Permission structure cleaned up. Access restored.
Activities
Emails:
18.02.25 08:59
Subject: No access to project folder “2025_Migration”
From: Contact Person 03
To: HELPDESK
Good morning
Several employees from the team “IT-Projekte” receive the following message when accessing the SharePoint folder
/Projects/2025_Migration
Access denied – You do not have the required permissions
Access was working until last week.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 03
18.02.25 09:06
Ticket TI-1569482 created
Assigned to: Employee 18
Status: In Progress
Notes:
18.02.25 09:22 – Remote analysis
• Access tested with affected user → reproducible
• SharePoint Online reachable
• Other project folders accessible
• Only folder “2025_Migration” affected
• Permission view in SharePoint shows: Access via group “SP_Project_IT”
Comparison with working user:
This user is member of “SP_Project_IT_Lead”
Suspicion: Group membership inconsistent
18.02.25 10:05 – Entra ID review
• User is member of Azure AD group “IT-Projekte”
• This group is nested in “SP_Project_IT”
• Notable: “SP_Project_IT” was newly created last week
History checked:
Old group “SP_Project_IT_v1” was deactivated
18.02.25 11:40 – Test measure
User added directly to “SP_Project_IT”
→ Access works immediately
After removing the direct assignment → access denied again
Conclusion: Nesting not functioning correctly
19.02.25 08:55 – Further analysis
Review of group structure:
IT-Projekte
└── SP_Project_IT_v1 (deactivated)
└── SP_Project_IT (new)
“IT-Projekte” was still nested in the old group, not in the new one.
Therefore, users did not receive effective permissions on the new SharePoint folder.
19.02.25 10:20 – Adjustment
• Nesting corrected
• “IT-Projekte” properly integrated into “SP_Project_IT”
• Old group removed
• Synchronization awaited
20.02.25 – Monitoring
Multiple users tested
Access consistently possible
25.02.25 14:30 – Customer feedback
Access works again for all employees without restriction.
Ticket closure
25.02.25 14:38
Dear Contact Person 03
The root cause was incorrect group nesting in Entra ID after the creation of the new SharePoint permission group.
The structure was cleaned up and tested.
Access has been restored.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This is a descriptive incident title reporting an outage and correction, without any directive language or suggestions for action.
groundedness_reason: The ticket states access to the project folder was denied due to incorrect group nesting in Entra ID involving the newly created “SP_Project_IT”; nesting was corrected and permissions cleaned up, after which access was restored.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: COMPLIANCE_TEAM / Azure AD Connect collision → consolidation + full sync (engineer‑led)

SOURCE 13:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically
Ticket number: TI-1583327
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 31
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.04.25 08:12
Closure
Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: The statement proposes specific actions—'consolidation + full sync (engineer-led)'—which reads as a recommendation or directive to address the Azure AD Connect collision.
groundedness_reason: The ticket describes a COMPLIANCE_TEAM existing as both cloud and on‑prem causing conflicts via Azure AD Connect; remediation lists consolidation (removing the old cloud group, cleaning On‑Prem), external guests managed as cloud‑only, and an Azure AD Connect full sync performed by engineering staff.

SOURCE 38:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – Access works only partially after permission change
Ticket number: TI-1571046
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 05
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Portal
Type: Incident
Created on: 04.03.25 13:18
Closure
Closure date: 11.03.25 16:02
Closure text: Root cause identified (Token caching & Conditional Access evaluation delay). Access stable after policy adjustment.
Activities
04.03.25 13:18
Ticket created via customer portal
Description:
Several employees report that after being added to the project “ERP-Upgrade”, they generally have access to SharePoint, but the folder
/Projects/ERP_Upgrade/Finance
is not displayed.
In some cases, access works for certain users after logging out and back in, but not for others.
Error message:
Access denied or item not found
04.03.25 13:32
Ticket assigned to: Employee 21
Status: In Progress
Notes:
04.03.25 14:05 – Initial analysis
• Affected users are members of Azure AD group “ERP_Finance”
• This group is a member of “SP_ERP_Project”
• SharePoint permissions correctly configured
• Comparison with working user shows identical group structure
Test:
Direct access via URL → partially successful
Notable:
Users with activated MFA requirement report issues more frequently.
04.03.25 15:40 – Conditional Access review
Tenant uses Conditional Access Policy:
“Require compliant device for Finance Resources”
Policy was adjusted on 28.02.
Some users access from private devices.
Device status in Entra ID partially “unknown”.
05.03.25 09:10 – Token analysis
Azure Sign-In Logs reviewed:
• Multiple entries with “Conditional Access not satisfied”
• At the same time, successful token issuance for SharePoint Root
Hypothesis:
Token was issued before group change → contains outdated claims
Users who had not fully logged out were working with cached tokens.
05.03.25 10:25 – Test measure
• User fully logged out (Office + browser)
• Browser cache cleared
• Device compliance status checked
Result:
Access possible afterward.
06.03.25 11:50 – In-depth analysis
Issue reoccurs for 2 users.
Difference identified:
These users access via Hybrid Azure AD Joined Devices.
Device synchronization was faulty.
Device compliance status was only updated after 6–8 hours.
07.03.25 09:30 – Actions
• Conditional Access Policy adjusted:
→ SharePoint project folder no longer included in Finance-specific CA rule
• Users re-authenticated
• Device sync triggered manually
10.03.25 – Monitoring
No further access issues reported.
Sign-In Logs unremarkable.
11.03.25 16:02 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 05
The root cause was not group nesting, but a delay in Conditional Access policy evaluation combined with cached authentication tokens.
After policy adjustment and renewed authentication, access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This statement reports an explanation and what engineers used to investigate intermittent access (sign-in logs), not a recommendation or suggested action.
groundedness_reason: The ticket analysis explicitly cites Azure Sign-In Logs showing 'Conditional Access not satisfied' and a hypothesis about issued tokens containing outdated claims; the closure states the root cause was a Conditional Access evaluation delay combined with cached authentication tokens.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?

03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21
Good morning
Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today.
Internal Teams calls function normally.
External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
03.09.25 09:08
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39.
03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Are all users affected or only specific ones?
Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones?

03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response
It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team.

It affects all external numbers.

03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note

Internal call → works.
External call → immediate termination after dialing.
Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”.
04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer
Good morning
We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration.
Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group?
Kind regards
04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response
Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week.

04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis
Users have:
• Teams license
• Calling Plan not active
• Direct Routing configured

Voice Routing Policy = “DR-Standard”
05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review
Get-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy shows that policy “Global” does not reference any PSTN Usage.
Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”.
05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer
Good day
We have identified that the affected users are currently assigned a different Voice Routing Policy than the other employees.
We are reviewing why this assignment was changed.
Kind regards
08.09.25 – Internal analysis
New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created.
Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group.
The internal document
“Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3”
states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy.
09.09.25 – Test phase
Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user.
Test call to mobile phone performed.
Call successful.
Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible.
10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team
Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago.
New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint.
11.09.25 – Adjustment
Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture.
Provisioning logic adjusted.
12.09.25 – Customer feedback
Good day
All affected users can dial external numbers again.
Longer calls also function normally.
Thank you for your support.
12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 21
The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring.
The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture.
External calls are now possible again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
